source_decisions.reason: Changes the trace/action evidence design and authoritative-receipt boundary.
Adds a distinct multi-task adversarial monitorability benchmark design.
source_decisions.passage_review: Abstract, introduction, design, stress tests, results, and limitations reviewed.

source_decisions.reason: Adds a concrete predictive-state and MPC interface plus explicit adoption residuals.
source_decisions.passage_review: Abstract, architecture, planning, results, and limitations reviewed.

source_decisions.reason: Adds the missing foundations boundary between finite records and an embedded agent/world.
source_decisions.passage_review: Primary abstract and canonical authors' full-text sections reviewed.

source_decisions.reason: Official 2026 control-overlay work remains a discussion draft and is redundant with stronger finalized custody/supply-chain comparators.
Due-diligence and provenance framing does not change the existing C-SCRM/SLSA/in-toto/SPDX/Croissant mechanism.
Preliminary JEPA/energy-based neighbors do not beat V-JEPA 2 as the mature empirical interface comparator for this pass.

ownership_test.interface_test: reasoning traces, predictive state, and embedded-agent limits already have unambiguous consumer/owner interfaces
ownership_test.invariant_test: each residual strengthens an existing invariant rather than creating a non-duplicative new one
ownership_test.artifact_test: trace records, substrate adoption records, plan/world-model records, evidence cells, and reference traces already own the needed artifacts
ownership_test.failure_mode_test: new chapters would duplicate trace/action mismatch, prediction error, sim-to-real, self-reference, and record-completeness failures
ownership_test.evidence_program_test: existing chapter validators and future campaign lanes can host the tests

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Output content provenance
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Internal provenance is mature, but no passage-reviewed primary C2PA/watermarking comparator was accepted in this bounded pass; retain as an event-driven source intake.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Interpretability as runtime control
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Interpretability remains evidence with bounded steering implications; the 2026 steering candidates were not passage-reviewed deeply enough to become runtime-control claims.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Memory consolidation lifecycle
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Consolidation, replay, proceduralization, and forgetting budgets already have owners and source-noted boundaries.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Calibration selective prediction and abstention
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Calibration, abstention, uncertainty, and reject routes are already explicit across Claim Ledgers, Readiness, Routing, and Verification Bandwidth.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Metacognition and capability self-knowledge
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: The capability self-model remains an event-driven intake; current self-assessment mentions do not warrant a new unreviewed source claim.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Collusion between components against oversight
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Shared dependencies, coordinated failure, role separation, and randomized or independent observation routes are already owned.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Oversight latency and fleet-scale monitoring
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Governance cost and latency are measured, but fleet-scale windows need a passage-reviewed deployment comparator before insertion.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Control tax as governance-tax comparator
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: The local governance-tax lane and its zero-denominator boundary are explicit; a new citation is unnecessary until a matched natural-work outcome exists.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Automated AI R&D acceleration thresholds
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: CAIS R&D automation and threshold commitments are already positioned; no new metric is claimed.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: STPA-style systematic hazard analysis
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: STPA is named as an open hazard-analysis route, but the candidate source was not accepted at passage depth in this pass.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Autonomous insider
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Agent-as-insider remains a valid security expansion but lacks a newly reviewed primary comparator and executable owner packet.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Hardware attestation trust-root class
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: TEE quotes, roots, verifier chains, stale evidence, and custody boundaries are central to the current chapter.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Open-weight release decision record
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Irreversible public release, disclosure scope, key release, revocation limits, and publication authority are already explicit.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Public governance graphs for market collusion
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Economic receipts are covered, but market-collusion governance graphs need a passage-reviewed primary source and systemic-risk owner.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Guaranteed-safe AI positioning
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: The record/spec versus model-safety boundary is already explicit; GSA positioning awaits paper-depth intake rather than relying on the older scan.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Machine unlearning and parametric deletion closure
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Full-state update, checkpoint authority, behavioral/influence/privacy/storage partitioning, and multiple primary unlearning comparators are present.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Prompt-injection impossibility as strongest objection
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Prompt-injection limits, architectural separation, AgentDojo, CAMEL, OWASP, and fail-closed external authority are already treated.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Scheming-oriented safety cases
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: The evaluations-based scheming safety-case source and defeater/countercase structure are already assigned and used.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Non-agentic Scientist AI position
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: The zero-execution-authority route is expressible, but Scientist AI needs primary passage review before a named position is added.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: CAIS prior-art mandate
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: CAIS is passage-reviewed in the opener, alignment, RSI, and reference architecture with a narrow typed-governance delta.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Behavior-specification practice crosswalk
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Constitutional predicates are mature, but the three-style and deliberative-alignment comparison remains a future passage-reviewed intake.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Formal-mathematics agents
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Proof producer/consumer interfaces exist; AlphaProof or another current capability comparator needs a separate primary-source pass.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: ARC-style program synthesis and test-time training
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Program synthesis, semantic IR, verifier-guided search, and test-time adaptation already have explicit owners.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Compute governance and distributed-training tension
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Federated compute governance/evasion dual remains a valid missing comparator but was not passage-reviewed in this bounded pass.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Introspection-training evidence
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: This duplicates the metacognition intake and remains deferred to one joint source-specific review.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Goal continuity under inherited trajectory pressure
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Objective fingerprints, periodic recontracting, drift, and inherited trajectory pressure are already explicit.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Causal and counterfactual reasoning
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: V-JEPA 2 adds predictive-state and MPC ownership while the text explicitly refuses to infer causal/interventional understanding; a causal-model source remains open.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Distribution shift OOD detection and runtime uncertainty
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: OOD, distribution shift, calibration, abstention, fallback, and quarantine are distributed across their current owners.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Temporal-logic monitoring and physical safety envelopes
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Bounded runtime monitoring is source-noted, but physical closed-loop safety envelopes remain outside the digital-adapter evidence scope.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Human-AI delegation calibration
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Weak-supervisor and escalation boundaries are present; rejector/router calibration needs its primary source pass.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Evaluator-access levels and environment custody
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Access, monitor visibility, evaluator provenance, model information, budgets, and false-negative residuals are explicit.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Domain-specific dangerous-capability profiles
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Thresholds are domain- and threat-specific and explicitly prohibit cross-domain generalization.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Post-deployment monitoring and incident disclosure
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Safety-case invalidation exists, but an end-to-end operational-resilience/incident owner remains outside the current 54-chapter spine.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: AIBOM lineage and signed build attestations
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Component inventory, model/data/build/training lineage, signing, attestations, derivatives, advisories, rebuild, and revocation are current core mechanisms.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Purpose limitation privacy budgets and internal-channel leakage
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Privacy evidence is partitioned for unlearning, but subject-rights and multi-user internal-channel governance still lack a dedicated owner/source packet.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Unlearning privacy paradox
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: MUSE and the benchmark critiques keep privacy separate from behavior; the specific before/after leakage source remains unreviewed.

tier_2_disposition_audit.title: Provider concentration substitutability and impact tolerances
tier_2_disposition_audit.rationale: Exit and fallback exist as general controls, but provider-concentration and resilience claims need a current primary-source owner packet.

reader_reconciliation.relationship: successor source edition; immutable v1.0 and v2.0 not modified

non_claims: Source insertion does not reproduce a paper result.
Citation count is not a completion metric.
No chapter-core claim moves above argument.
The v2.1 reader source is not a released format artifact.
No model quality, monitorability, causal world model, corrigibility, safety, AGI, or ASI result is established.
